Package main.models

package main.models
  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    main
     
    main.enums
     
    main.states
     
    main.utils
     
  • Classes
    Class
    Description
    Admin
    Represents an administrative user with elevated system privileges.
    Admin.Builder
    Builder implementation dedicated to constructing Admin instances.
    Client
    Represents a client account, extending RegistrableUser with commercial metrics such as sector and business scale.
    Client.Builder
    Builder pattern implementation for cleanly generating Client instances.
    Employee
    Represents an employee within the system, expanding on RegistrableUser with professional details like specialization and start date.
    Employee.Builder
    Builder pattern implementation for creating structured Employee instances.
    Equipment
    Tracks physical hardware or assets submitted for maintenance, managing manufacturing timelines, serial codes, and repair tracking fields.
    Log
    Audit trail data record mapping specific events or technical system changes driven by explicit user activity.
    Notification
    System alert message directed to targeted groups or single entities.
    Part
    Represents a spare part that can be used in equipment repairs, tracked with a stock quantity.
    RegistrableUser
    Base class for users who register additional contact/identification details beyond the common User fields — namely Employee and Client.
    RegistrableUser.Builder<T extends RegistrableUser.Builder<T>>
    Base builder for RegistrableUser subclasses, adding the registrable-specific fields (NIF, phone, address) on top of User.Builder.
    Repair
    Tracks an individual technical maintenance job lifecycle, documenting cost metrics, scheduling milestones, and diagnostic details.
    User
    Abstract base class representing a generic user in the system.
    User.Builder<T extends User.Builder<T>>
    Abstract builder pattern implementation to support fluent inheritance across different user sub-types.
